card.intuition: The phonebook of the internet — turning human-friendly domain names into machine-readable IP addresses.
card.analogy: Looking up a contact in your phone: you search for 'Mom' and your phone finds the number '555-0199'.
when_to_use: Connecting to any server using a domain name instead of a hardcoded IP
Setting up load balancing via DNS (Round Robin)
Configuring email servers using MX records
gotchas: DNS propagation can take hours — changes aren't always instant due to recursive caching
Root servers don't store individual website IPs; they point you to the TLD servers (.com, .org, etc.)

exercise.prompt: Order the DNS resolution steps from browser to IP.
exercise.starter_code:
def dns_lookup_flow():
    steps = [
        "Browser checks local cache",
        "OS checks /etc/hosts",
        "____ Resolver (usually ISP)",
        "____ Name Server",
        "____ Name Server (e.g., .com)",
        "____ Name Server (e.g., cloudflare)"
    ]
    return steps
hints: The ISP runs the Recursive resolver
The 'dot' at the end of a domain starts at the Root
The final server that knows the IP is Authoritative
exercise.test_code:
steps = dns_lookup_flow()
assert "Recursive" in steps[2]
assert "Root" in steps[3]
assert "TLD" in steps[4]
assert "Authoritative" in steps[5]
print("PASS")
